title: DodagConfig::from_bytes reads data[10] but field is reserved
description:
File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/message.rs
Lines: 243-256

In DodagConfig::from_bytes(), the code reads def_lifetime from data[11], skipping data[10] (reserved). However, the code structure is slightly misleading and the field mapping could be error-prone:

```rust
pub fn from_bytes(data: &[u8]) -> Result<Self, RplError> {
    // ...
    Ok(Self {
        dio_int_doublings: data[1],
        dio_int_min: data[2],
        dio_redundancy_const: data[3],
        max_rank_increase: u16::from_be_bytes([data[4], data[5]]),
        min_hop_rank_increase: u16::from_be_bytes([data[6], data[7]]),
        ocp: u16::from_be_bytes([data[8], data[9]]),
        def_lifetime: data[11],  // data[10] is reserved, skipped
        lifetime_unit: u16::from_be_bytes([data[12], data[13]]),
    })
}
```

While this is technically correct per RFC 6550 Section 6.7.6, comparing with write_to shows an asymmetry:
- from_bytes skips data[0] (A/PCS flags) but doesn't document this
- from_bytes skips data[10] (reserved) 

This is more of a code clarity issue than a functional bug, but the inconsistency could lead to maintenance errors.

Severity: P4 - cosmetic/clarity issue, not a functional bug.
